title: circaea lutetiana f. ericetorum
intro: Circaea lutetiana f. ericetorum, also known as the wood sorrel, is a subspecies of perennial herb that belongs to the wood sorrel family (Gentianaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect the skin, as well as reduce swelling and pain. The health benefits of wood sorrel include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Wood sorrel can be prepared in various herbal remedies, such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
